sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: The upcoming clash between Cagliari and Udinese is set for May 3, 2025, at 13:00 at the Unipol Domus in Cagliari. This Round 35 encounter sees Cagliari, currently 14th in the league with 33 points, hosting Udinese, who sit slightly higher in 12th place with 41 points. With both teams in the mid-table region, this match could be crucial for securing a more comfortable position as the season nears its end. The referee, Ermanno Feliciani, is known for averaging 4.2 yellow cards per game, which could influence the physicality of the match.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at their last five matchups, the head-to-head record between Cagliari and Udinese is fairly balanced with 1 win for Cagliari, 2 draws, and 2 wins for Udinese. Their most recent encounter on October 25, 2024, saw Udinese triumph 2-0 at home, with Jesper Karlström earning the Player of the Match title with a stellar 7.8 Sofascore rating. Historically, matches between these two sides tend to be low-scoring, with 4 out of their last 5 games having less than 2.5 goals. Additionally, Cagliari has failed to keep a clean sheet in their last 3 meetings against Udinese, which could be a concern for their defense.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Udinese are in a rough patch, currently on a streak of 7 games without a win and failing to score in their last 4 matches. They’ve also been the first to concede in 5 of their last 6 games, indicating defensive vulnerabilities. On the other hand, Cagliari have been involved in games with fewer than 4.5 cards in 9 of their last 10 matches, suggesting a more disciplined approach. Both teams have a tendency for low corner counts, with 8 of Cagliari’s last 10 and 6 of their last 7 head-to-head games featuring fewer than 10.5 corners.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Cagliari
sections.text: Cagliari will rely heavily on forward Roberto Piccoli, who has scored 9 goals this season with an expected goals tally of 10.34. Another standout is Tommaso Augello, a defender who ranks 2nd in the league for big chances created with 12, alongside 6 assists, making him a vital creative force from the back with a 6.91 average rating. Midfielder Răzvan Marin, with 3 goals and 2 assists, and Gaetano Gianluca, with 2 goals and 4 assists, will also be crucial in driving Cagliari’s midfield dynamism.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Udinese
sections.text: Udinese boast Oumar Solet, a defender with an impressive average Sofascore rating of 7.34, ranking 4th in the league, and also ranking 5th for percentage of long balls at 9.1%. Midfielder Sandi Lovrić, with 2 goals and 3 assists, and forward Keinan Davis, with 2 goals and 1 assist, will be key to breaking their scoring drought. Jesper Karlström, who shone in their last meeting with Cagliari, remains a player to watch with his 6.77 average rating and 2 assists this season.

sections.subtitle: Missing Players Impact
sections.text: Cagliari will miss defender Yerry Mina, who has a strong average rating of 7.02 despite not scoring this season. Udinese, meanwhile, face significant absences with forwards Lorenzo Lucca, who has 10 goals, and Florian Thauvin, with 8 goals and 3 assists, sidelined. These absences could severely hamper Udinese’s attacking threat, especially given their current streak of 4 games without a goal.

sections.subtitle: Statistical Insights - Cagliari
sections.text: Cagliari have scored 35 goals this season, ranking 14th in the league, while conceding 49 goals, also 14th for least goals conceded. They rank impressively 3rd for goals vs expected goals difference at 5.2234, showing they overperform in attack. Their strength in headers is notable, with 9 goals from headers ranking 4th league-wide, and they create a solid 75 big chances, ranking 7th. However, their conversion rate of big chances stands at a low 25.33%, placing them 20th in the league.

sections.subtitle: Statistical Insights - Udinese
sections.text: Udinese’s goal distribution shows a knack for scoring late, with 20.83% of their goals coming between the 46th and 60th minutes and another 20.83% between the 76th and 90th minutes. Their timing could pose a threat to Cagliari if the game remains tight. However, their recent inability to score, coupled with a tendency to concede first in 5 of their last 6 matches, suggests they might struggle to capitalize on these late opportunities.

sections.subtitle: Standout Historical Performer
sections.text: In the last 5 matchups against Cagliari, Udinese’s Alexis Sánchez has been a standout, averaging a Sofascore rating of 7.38 across 3 starts. With 1 goal and 2 assists in these games, Sánchez has proven to be a consistent threat against Cagliari’s defense. His experience and attacking output could be a difference-maker if he features prominently in this match.
